title: Why Your Sleep Is Out of Sync (And the Fix)
description: Long-form YouTube explainer connecting circadian disruption with hormonal imbalance, with three practical resets.
hook: Your sleep didn't break — your hormones did.

cta.text: Subscribe for the rest of the sleep series

keyMessage: Most sleep problems are circadian, not behavioral.

strategy.keyMessage: Most sleep problems are circadian, not behavioral.
strategy.ctaText: Subscribe for the rest of the sleep series

creativePlan.hook: Your sleep didn't break — your hormones did.
creativePlan.musicNotes: Sparse synth bed under voiceover; silence under research overlays

videoLong.hook: If you keep waking up at 3 a.m. and you're over 40, your sleep didn't break — your hormones did. In the next 12 minutes I'll show you why, and the three changes that actually move the needle.

sequenceBlocks.description: Cold open: 3 a.m. clock shot, voiceover establishes the universal experience. Promise the payoff (3 fixes in 12 minutes).
Section 1: The mechanism. Cortisol and progesterone curves; what perimenopause does to them. On-screen graph + lower-third citations.
Section 2: Fix #1 — light hygiene. Morning sunlight protocol with timing specifics. B-roll of subject going outside; overlay the protocol as a checklist.
Section 3: Fix #2 — temperature gradient. Bedroom temperature, hot showers before bed. Show a thermometer beat, then narrate.
Section 4: Fix #3 — magnesium glycinate timing. Specific protocol; what to look for on a label. Hold up a real bottle.
Recap the three fixes as bullet text. Subscribe pitch tied to the next episode (cortisol-aware morning routine).

videoLong.voiceoverNotes: Calm, slow, lower register on the mechanism section. Step UP energy in the fixes section to keep it actionable. Avoid jargon — define every clinical term on first use.
